name: Printful API
what_it_does: Print-on-demand and dropshipping platform API for creating custom products, managing orders, and handling fulfillment and shipping without inventory.
use_cases: Agents automating creation and listing of custom-printed merchandise
E-commerce integrations that fulfill print orders on demand
Automating order placement and tracking for custom apparel and accessories
Syncing product catalogs with Shopify, Etsy, or WooCommerce stores
Building custom storefronts backed by Printful fulfillment
not_for: Bulk or wholesale manufacturing — per-unit cost is higher than traditional printing
Products requiring complex custom packaging or branding inserts at scale
Time-sensitive fulfillment where 3-7 day production windows are unacceptable
best_when: An agent needs to run a custom merchandise store or automate product creation and order fulfillment without managing inventory or shipping logistics.
avoid_when: You need sub-2-day fulfillment or are doing high-volume orders where unit economics matter.

auth.notes: Supports both OAuth 2.0 (recommended for multi-store integrations) and long-lived private tokens (API keys) for single-store automation. OAuth scopes are granular per resource type.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No monthly fee; API access included with free account
pricing.paid_tiers: Pay per order fulfilled; no monthly subscription required. Printful Plus ($24.99/mo) adds branding features.
pricing.notes: No monthly API fee. Costs are purely per-order (product cost + shipping). Free account sufficient for development and testing with sample orders.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: JSON error responses with code and message fields. Errors are reasonably descriptive but some validation errors require cross-referencing catalog documentation.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Order creation is not idempotent by default; agents should check existing orders before creating to avoid duplicates.
known_agent_gotchas: Product catalog changes frequently — agents should not hardcode variant IDs
Order creation requires valid shipping address validation before submission
Mockup generation (for listing images) is async — agents must poll or use webhooks
Rate limit of 120 req/min can be hit during bulk catalog syncs
Credit card must be on file before orders can be fulfilled

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. OAuth 2.0 with resource-level scopes. Private tokens (long-lived) available for simpler integrations. GDPR compliant. Webhook events should be validated using X-Printful-Signature header.
